text:
Cruelty × Determination × Climax (冷酷×決意×, Reikoku × Getsui × Kuraimakusu) is the 91 st  episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime and the 13 th episode of the Greed Island final OVA series. It was released on DVD on August 18 th , 2004.

Overview

Gon has defeated Genthru by creating a deep pit and dropping a boulder into it, which trapped Genthru and knocked him unconscious using his Janken ability. Following the battle, Killua and Biscuit heal everyone, including Gon, despite Goreinu's objections. With the help of Goreinu and Tsezguerra, who generously gave him all their cards, Gon has now collected a total of 99 cards. As the players in Greed Island gather around Gon and the others, they prepare for the upcoming quiz, where the player with the top score will win the final card: the Ruler's Blessing.

Synopsis

Unable to use Little Flower due to being soaked in gasoline, Genthru switches to Countdown. After meeting the required conditions, he prepares to activate it against Gon, warning him of its immense destructive power. However, Gon remains calm, convinced that the conditions now favor him.

Gon summons an overwhelming amount of aura, leaving Genthru stunned by its intensity. Watching in disbelief, Genthru assumes Gon is about to unleash a devastating attack. Instead, Gon uses Janken: Rock, slamming the ground with enormous force and creating a deep pit that drags both of them down.

As the dust clears, Genthru observes the pit’s depth and concludes that neither of them can escape. Believing that Gon is trapped alongside him, he scans the area, only to suddenly realize, with growing alarm, that Gon is nowhere to be found.

Suddenly, Gon calls out to Genthru. Upon hearing Gon’s voice, Genthru turns around and sees him standing on a man-made path inside the massive pit. Gon summons a card and throws it into the air above the pit. The card transforms into a huge boulder, perfectly sized to fit snugly into the pit but small enough to fall through.

Realizing the danger, Genthru, now in a tight spot, has only seconds to maneuver and avoid being crushed by the falling boulder. As the boulder slams into the ground, Genthru narrowly escapes, gasping for breath.

Before he can fully recover, a sudden spark of light flashes in front of him. Gon is now charging his Janken: Rock with a massive amount of aura. Worried with fear, Genthru realizes he is powerless to defend himself. In desperation, he begs for his life, repeatedly surrendering.

However, the resolute Gon delivers a powerful punch with all his strength, knocking Genthru unconscious. Gon performs the "Osu" and then exits the pit while dragging Genthru with him.

After making it outside, Gon immediately Contacts Killua and Biscuit to inform them of his victory over Genthru. However, his voice is unclear, and Killua and Biscuit struggle to understand him. Biscuit suspects that Gon’s throat may have been damaged during the battle, just as she had anticipated.

After some time, Genthru regains consciousness and finds himself tied up on the ground alongside Bara and Sub. Opening his eyes, he sees Killua and Biscuit standing over him. They order him to summon his binder and demand that he return all the stolen cards from the people he and his group had robbed. Genthru agrees but insists they must first heal Bara, who is severely injured and unconscious. Killua reassures him, and Biscuit adds that it was part of their plan from the start.

With no further objections, Genthru hands over his binder. Killua and Biscuit summon an Angel's Breath card to heal Gon’s injuries. The trio watches in awe as the magic takes effect, fully restoring Gon’s body, including his severed hand and voice. Overjoyed by his recovery, Gon, Killua, and Biscuit celebrate as the Angel’s Breath bids farewell and vanishes.

At Gon’s signal, Biscuit prepares to use another Angel’s Breath clone card to heal Bara. She activates the card, and it successfully heals Bara, who slowly regains consciousness. However, upon using another clone to heal Genthru, the card disintegrates. The game’s Moderator announces that the maximum usage limit for Angel’s Breath has been reached, rendering the clone ineffective. Confused and uneasy, Biscuit begins to question if she made a mistake.

Killua steps in, explaining that it’s likely the work of Goreinu. He clarifies that Goreinu must have increased his claim on the Angel’s Breath cards using Clone or Transform. To further illustrate, Killua provides an example: if two Angel’s Breath cards are used, Goreinu would automatically claim two more, exhausting the card’s availability.

The trio receives a call from Goreinu and decides to meet up. After Goreinu joins the trio, he objects to healing Genthru and his team, telling them they have killed many people and won't be trading any cards for their sake. Killua then asks if it's him, to which Goreinu replies that he won't trade it with Killua. Instead, he will just give it to him. Killua tells Goreinu that he killed more people than Genthru. His team further elaborates that everyone who comes to Greed Island understands the risks of getting here, even comparing those who killed were not even prepared to die, unlike the one who got killed by Genthru's, Killua continues to persuade him, Goreinu still doesn't agree, and the group kept talking about this thing. Gon steps in and informs Goreinu that the trio had planned everything before even confronting Genthru; this was part of their strategy to heal everyone after their success. However, he acknowledges Goreinu's feelings about the situation and considers his perspective regarding Genthru's team. Aware that Goreinu is unlikely to change his stance, he then approaches Killua, apologizing and asking him to wait for a moment. Killua reassures Gon that he is fine, stating that his hands no longer cause him significant discomfort. Gon then approaches Genthru and states that they cannot use any more clones; instead, he will utilize the original card to heal Genthru's team. Sub then asks them to apply the card to Genthru, expressing concern for his comrade. Gon uses the card to heal Genthru without hesitation, despite Goreinu's objections.

After healing Genthru, the trio decides to travel to Masadora. Just as they prepare to set off, Goreinu stops them and announces that he will give them all his Angel's Breath cards. He explains that before meeting with them, he and Tsezguerra's team had agreed to give all their cards to the trio as they planned to quit the game.

While Killua receives treatment, Goreinu elaborates further on his arrangement with Tsezguerra, detailing the compensation he will receive due to Battera's breach of contract. He informs them that he intends to share this compensation with the trio. Biscuit is delighted by this news, while Gon and Killua insist they do not need the money, much to Biscuit's annoyance. In a humorous reprimand, she scolds the two of them.

The discussion shifts to the task of collecting all 99 cards, leaving just one remaining: the coveted 000 card. All eyes are on the trio as everyone inside the game eagerly anticipates the outcome of this achievement. Gon places each card in its designated spot in the binder while Goreinu, Killua, and Biscuit watch him intently.

Once all the cards are secured in the binder, the Game Moderator announces that a player has collected all 99 designated cards. She informs everyone that there will be a celebration after the next ten minutes, inviting all Greed Island players to participate in a quiz containing 100 questions related to the designated cards. The player with the highest score can claim the Ruler's Blessing card.

As the trio discusses the upcoming quiz, players from all directions converge on their location. A brief tension fills the air, prompting Gon, Killua, Biscuit, and Goreinu to remain alert. However, a group approaches the trio, declaring they come in peace and wish to negotiate. They express their interest in acquiring the Ruler's Blessing card and will trade it to Gon for money if they emerge on top.

Killua, understanding the situation, confirms with the nearby players whether they share the same motives, to which they all agree. Gon expresses his desire to answer the quiz questions individually, and Killua challenges him, stating that whoever scores the lowest will face a consequence. Gon enthusiastically accepts the challenge, and Biscuit observes them with a smile, delighted by their happiness. She recognizes they are the only ones enjoying the game, continuing to find happiness despite facing a life-and-death situation earlier.

Meanwhile, a mysterious figure clad in a hooded cloak approaches Genthru. The stranger touches him and recites the words that deactivate Genthru's Countdown, which had been planted on him.

As the quiz time approaches, Gon, Killua, and the rest prepare for the challenge ahead.
